conditions.notes: Used for analysis of fatty acid ethoxylates and PEG samples. The text states this composition corresponds to the Critical Adsorption Point (CAP) for PEG.
conditions.evidence_text: Figure 3a shows the LCCC separation of the same samples as in Figure 2 and PEG 600 on the Onyx C18 monolith in 90 % acetone – water, which corresponds to the CAP for PEG.
Right side: Off-line two-dimensional separation: First dimension: LCCC in 90 % methanol with focussing of fractions on a FAD column
conditions.notes: This is the first dimension of an off-line 2D-LC setup (LCCC-LEAC). The text identifies the first dimension as LCCC in 90% methanol. The column is referred to as 'FAD column' which likely refers to the Onyx C18 monolith used in other LCCC experiments, but the text does not explicitly link the name 'FAD' to the specific column specs in this sentence. The stationary phase chemistry is inferred from the general LCCC setup described in the experimental section (Onyx C18).
